findings.text: Not a pocket. It's worked into the grain storage itself, floor to rafter.
findings.deletionTest: It's worked into the grain storage itself, floor to rafter.
findings.reason: The negative half is dead scaffolding; the committed clause carries the whole point.
findings.suggestedFix: It's worked into the grain storage itself, floor to rafter.

findings.text: Not the way a dock hand watches for the next cart. The way a sentry watches for someone he doesn't want coming through.
findings.deletionTest: The way a sentry watches for someone he doesn't want coming through.
findings.reason: The sentence lands on the sentry comparison; the rejected dock-hand comparison only delays it.
findings.suggestedFix: The way a sentry watches for someone he doesn't want coming through.

findings.text: Not caution. Control.
findings.reason: The fragment refuses one noun just to land on the real one.
findings.suggestedFix: Control.

findings.text: Not hope exactly. Direction.
findings.reason: The sentence survives and sharpens once the negated fake-out is removed.
findings.suggestedFix: Direction.

findings.text: Not calm. Readiness.
findings.reason: This is the same two-beat negation reveal the guide marks as a kill.
findings.suggestedFix: Readiness.

findings.text: Not nausea. Recognition.
findings.reason: The second fragment carries the meaning; the first only stages it.
findings.suggestedFix: Recognition.

findings.text: Not plague on a countryside road. Not a girl coughing blood in a roadside hut. Gloved hands in lamplight working poison into grain that would be loaded before dawn and hauled to bakeries, market stalls, tables where people would eat without knowing what sat in them.
findings.deletionTest: Gloved hands in lamplight working poison into grain that would be loaded before dawn and hauled to bakeries, market stalls, tables where people would eat without knowing what sat in them.
findings.reason: The chain refuses two comparisons before naming the concrete image that already does the work.
findings.suggestedFix: Gloved hands in lamplight working poison into grain that would be loaded before dawn and hauled to bakeries, market stalls, tables where people would eat without knowing what sat in them.

findings.text: This is not crude. This is not hedge-work.
findings.deletionTest: Someone with high-arcanum training built this and hid it inside grain.
findings.reason: The paired refusals merely tee up the concrete claim that follows.
findings.suggestedFix: Someone with high-arcanum training built this and hid it inside grain.

findings.text: This is not contamination. It is not an accident.
findings.deletionTest: Someone with high-arcanum training built a weapon that uses the grain trade as the road in and the human body as the furnace.
findings.reason: The negated preamble delays the actual claim that the plague mechanism is deliberate.
findings.suggestedFix: This was deliberate.

findings.text: Not because the question was wrong. Because nobody here had the right to guess.
findings.deletionTest: Because nobody here had the right to guess.
findings.reason: The first clause is a source-swap fake-out; the causal sentence stands on its own.
findings.suggestedFix: Because nobody here had the right to guess.

findings.text: That changed everything the road had taught them.
findings.subject: road
findings.realAgent: the villages, depots, and plague evidence they had already passed on the road
findings.reason: The road is laundering the lessons supplied by concrete evidence and prior observation.
findings.suggestedFix: That changed everything they had learned on the road.

findings.text: if the road had taught them anything true
findings.subject: road
findings.realAgent: their accumulated field evidence from villages, depots, and bodies along the route
findings.reason: The clause gives instruction to geography instead of to the evidence Sylvanas has been reading all chapter.
findings.suggestedFix: if they had learned anything true on the road

findings.text: the city ground poison into bread
findings.subject: city
findings.realAgent: the millers, bakers, and cult handlers working the grain chain
findings.reason: The sentence gives the city's people-and-infrastructure labor to the abstract noun city.
findings.suggestedFix: millers and bakers ground poison into bread

findings.text: His eyes went over her the way a street officer's would: hands, belt, stance, face.
findings.subject: eyes
findings.reason: The body part is doing the assessment that belongs to the man.
findings.suggestedFix: He looked her over the way a street officer would: hands, belt, stance, face.

findings.text: One finger traced down the page.
findings.subject: finger
findings.realAgent: Jaina tracing the page while she reasons through the contamination path
findings.reason: The finger steals the physical action from the character performing it.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina traced one finger down the page.

findings.text: a map that might never finish
findings.subject: map
findings.reason: The unfinished work belongs to Havel, not to the map as an autonomous agent.
findings.suggestedFix: a map Havel might never finish

findings.text: The district knows my face.
findings.subject: district
findings.realAgent: the workers and regulars who see Havel every day
findings.reason: The district is standing in for the people inside it who actually recognize Havel.
findings.suggestedFix: The workers here know my face.

findings.text: The next three blocks told the same story at different volumes.
findings.subject: blocks
findings.realAgent: the workers, carts, broken sightlines, and repeated behavior Sylvanas is reading
findings.reason: Concrete evidence is present, but the sentence launders it onto the blocks.
findings.suggestedFix: The next three blocks showed the same pattern at different volumes.

findings.text: The route lived in his body.
findings.subject: route
findings.realAgent: the handler carrying a memorized route in trained muscle memory
findings.reason: The memorization belongs to the handler, not to the route as an inhabiting agent.
findings.suggestedFix: He carried the route in his body.

findings.text: The room went still.
findings.subject: room
findings.realAgent: the people in the inspection office stopping at Jaina's explanation
findings.reason: The stillness belongs to the people in the room, not to the room itself.
findings.suggestedFix: Everyone in the room went still.

findings.text: She marked the guard pattern as they walked.
findings.reason: This is one of four chapter-level mental uses of marked; the chapter is past the hard cap and this is the weakest generic instance.
findings.suggestedFix: She read the guard pattern as they walked.

findings.text: Sylvanas looked at Jaina.
findings.reason: The chapter is far past the eye/gaze cap, and this is a bare generic look beat with no character-specific value.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas turned to Jaina.

findings.text: the workers you've marked
findings.reason: This is another mental-use marked instance beyond the chapter cap, and the meaning stays clearer with a more specific verb.
findings.suggestedFix: the workers you've identified

findings.text: Havel held her eyes for a long moment while the grey light thinned toward dusk.
findings.reason: The beat routes tension through another generic eye-hold after the chapter has already exhausted that repertoire.
findings.suggestedFix: Havel did not answer for a long moment while the grey light thinned toward dusk.

findings.text: Havel watched without comment, one hand still near the truncheon.
findings.reason: The chapter is already over the tool cap for watched, and this is the weakest generic stage-direction use.
findings.suggestedFix: Havel stood without comment, one hand still near the truncheon.

findings.text: He looked at her.
findings.reason: Another generic look beat that adds no more than turn-to-speaker mechanics.
findings.suggestedFix: He turned to her.

findings.text: People always looked at the militia captain.
findings.reason: This repeats the chapter's default eye-routing for attention instead of naming the social effect more directly.
findings.suggestedFix: The militia captain always pulled attention.

findings.text: Sylvanas sat, wiped soot from her palms onto her coat, and met Jaina's eyes across the table.
findings.reason: The chapter's eye-contact beats are already over cap, and this instance is replaceable with a cleaner orientation beat.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas sat, wiped soot from her palms onto her coat, and faced Jaina across the table.

findings.text: The room went still.
findings.reason: This one-sentence paragraph sits inside the tool-flagged three-paragraph The opener run and is the weakest of the cluster.
findings.suggestedFix: Everyone in the room went still.

summary.verdictReason: Fail: pass A found 8 moderate agency-laundering kills plus 21 mild kills across negation addiction and recurring tics. The chapter also carries structural rhythm concerns and a structural texture deficit that amplify the pass-A problems.

textureMetrics.interpretation: PARTICIPIAL: 3.8% → target 12.0% (score 47/100). Add ', Ving' phrases. | COMPOUND: 4.0% → target 10.0% (score 55/100). Join related clauses with conjunctions. | EMDASH: 1.0% → target 4.0% (score 72/100). Use for mid-sentence pivots. | SHORT SENTENCES: 58.9% above ceiling 55.0%; combine adjacent shorts.
